references.reason: The intake table makes four evidence layers visible before any treatment decision.
Observed signs remain separate from interpretation and diagnosis.
The ledger preserves each patient's own wording without clinical overwrite.
Tewi's paw-stamped cards make misleading symptoms visually quarantineable.
The triage mechanic separates trace layers and pauses safely on loss.
The journal records competence as separation, consent, and bounded uncertainty.
The consent desk keeps the closed Lens case visible throughout both refusals.
A complete refusal record leaves its reason blank without treating the chart as incomplete.
The closed case makes non-action after refusal visually explicit.
The optional return card expires without creating medical or relationship debt.
The consent branch records limited scope and continued permission to withdraw.
The refusal branch proves that care and route access continue without penalty.
One table keeps Kosuzu's ordinary next task and Kaguya's cosmic chart simultaneously visible.
The complete chart preserves a real cosmic problem instead of reducing it to spectacle.
Return slips make the small injury matter through the human work immediately following care.
The sealed packet opens only within Kosuzu's stated consent and contains no real-world treatment instructions.
The planning board keeps consent, next use, and cosmic evidence visible without equating scale with worth.
The journal distinguishes equal attention from identical duration or procedure.
The boundary desk keeps the sealed sample, withdrawn form, and accepted blank result visible during Eirin's anger.
A round container and intact high-contrast seal distinguish the unopened sample without depicting an actionable experiment procedure.
The initially uncrossed form receives a thick diagonal X only after withdrawal, making the player's boundary action irreversible and readable in one bit.
A titled result sheet with surrounding ruled fields and one deliberately blank central result cell reads as accepted uncertainty rather than missing art.
The journal preserves Eirin's anger, the withdrawn offer, and continuation without route lockout.
A closed rest door, still transom lamp, morning tray, and completed sandglass make uninterrupted rest readable without intruding on Eirin's room.
An open ledger uses paired duty columns, distinct signatures, and one protagonist-marked morning row to show competence transferred and the player's boundary action remained visible.
A shallow tray with Kaguya's fan-shaped seal holds the face-down Archive card until morning and cannot be mistaken for the ledger.
A vertical door card uses one thick black emergency band above thin routine lines so the no-knock threshold remains legible in one bit.
A tall emptied sandglass beside the unchanged closed door proves the chosen rest period completed rather than the scene merely cutting away.
The journal records chosen rest, bounded handoff, two safely deferred interruptions, and trust repaired through practice.
The audit table opposes the Archive's rigid three-zero form with Eirin's open revision ledger and an unmistakably empty patient chair.
Three stacked zeroes use hourglass, question, and pencil headers, then receive one high-contrast PATIENT ABSENT stamp across all columns so they cannot read as a positive score.
A consent row retains broken rule lines and half-aligned checkbox corners at its cut edge, making deliberate deletion visible rather than resembling UI cropping.
A rigid square checksum replaces the handwritten patient name and visually demonstrates that a changing person became a fixed record.
One ruled line remains deliberately open beneath completed entries in Eirin's ledger, preserving a readable place for later correction.
The journal records Eirin's autonomous refusal, Kaguya's non-universal immortality, and the absence of any sample, procedure, or substitute target.
A small private table places the finite ledger, its key, and one appointment card on the protagonist's side while Eirin leaves every intent field unmarked.
A visibly finite set of page tabs and a solid back cover communicate an ordinary lifespan without predicting or counting down a death.
A distinct key rests beyond the table's center line on the protagonist's side, making ownership and control legible in one bit.
One small card has exactly one date box and two separate signature marks, promising a chosen next appointment rather than an infinite calendar.
The friendship journal preserves the ordinary-life ledger, patient ownership, per-field consent, and a legitimate final page.
The romance journal records explicit consent to one chosen appointment without claiming forever.
The declined journal proves that no leaves appointment access, familiar regard, ledger ownership, and care intact.
The postponed journal separates a clinical date from any deadline or fee for naming the relationship.
The undecided journal preserves an unclaimed future as valid uncertainty rather than a symptom awaiting correction.
